_comment: KritiKaal Leads Hunter — Operator-controlled agent tuning (Sprint 7.5).
_comment2: This file is OPTIONAL. Delete it or leave it absent to run all defaults.
_comment3: All keys are optional — omit any key to keep its default value.

global._comment: Applies to every agent unless a per-agent override takes precedence.
global._comment_max_iter: Default circuit-breaker. Reduce to 2-3 for quick smoke-test runs.
global._comment_exhaustion: Stop an agent when < 10% of results are new domains. Raise to 0.20 for stricter pruning.
global._comment_max_new_leads: Hard cap on new domains classified this run (across ALL agents). 0 = unlimited. Example: set to 50 for a fast focused run or to cap API spend. Domains beyond this cap are skipped (not lost — they will be found again next run).

agents._comment: Keys must exactly match the 'name' field in AGENT_REGISTRY (live_run.py).

Core Leather._comment: Primary vertical — keep at full iterations.

Lateral Industries._comment: Highest query volume (~39 queries). Safe to reduce to 3 after market matures.

Competitor Lookalike._comment: Two-phase agent. 3 iterations is usually enough once competitor pages are mined.

Gov Procurement._comment_disable_example: Set enabled:false to skip entirely for a quick run (saves ~3 Serper batches).

Social-Only._comment: Facebook resolver — market exhausts quickly. 2 iterations is usually sufficient.

Purchase Intent._comment: Active RFQ signals. Add custom Hebrew RFQ queries below to probe new categories.

quick_smoke_test._description: Disable two slow agents, cap iterations at 2 globally for a fast run.

cost_capped_run._description: Classify at most 30 new domains this run to keep API spend under ~$0.05.

purchase_intent_focus._description: Disable all agents except Purchase Intent + Core Leather.
